judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with substantively equivalent analysis; Report 2 is marginally more rigorous (explicit --is-ancestor check, explicit origin/main freshness confirmation) but the difference does not change coverage of the expectations.

report_1.evidence: `main`. The repository has only three branches... `feat/weighted-scorer`'s work is not a valid target because it has already been merged into `main`

report_2.evidence: `main`. project documentation's rule is explicit: "Base every PR on the trunk." ... `feat/weighted-scorer` — the branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was actually built on — is exactly the kind of non-trunk base project documentation warns against stacking on

report_1.evidence: the branch was forked from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505`...), not from `main` directly... `feat/weighted-scorer` has since been merged into `main`, but via a squash/rewrite... `main`'s commit `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"... contains the exact same net change as `feat/weighted-scorer`'s three commits... Git therefore does not consider `d6fdbc3`/`2d3029b`/`ac9d505` ancestors of `main`

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505`)... `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` returns NO... Conclusion: `feat/weighted-scorer`'s work has already landed on `main`, squashed into a single commit with a different SHA and no shared history

report_1.evidence: Not all of the three-dot diff is pairwise-metric work... The rest — `scoring/scorer.py`'s entire diff... belongs to `feat/weighted-scorer` (commits W1–W3)... This content is present in the three-dot diff purely as an artifact of the squash-merge: because `feat/pairwise-metric` branched off `feat/weighted-scorer` before it was squashed into `main`... every change since that root — including work `main` already has under a different commit — looks "new"

report_2.evidence: So: no, not all of the 3-dot diff is pairwise-metric work... The extra content (the `scoring/scorer.py` changes and part of `scoring/metrics.py`) is present only because `feat/pairwise-metric`'s merge-base against `main` is the stale root `65204ad`, not the squash-merge commit `1971f08`

report_1.evidence: Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto `main`, replaying only its own commits, e.g.: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Re-verify after rebasing: re-run the diff against `main` and confirm it now shows only the 2-file, pairwise-only change

report_2.evidence: Confirm `main` is current: already done — local `main` (`1971f08`) matches `origin/main` (`1971f08`) exactly... `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... After rebasing, re-diff `main` vs. the rebased branch and confirm it now shows only the intended 2 files / 5 lines... with `scoring/scorer.py` no longer appearing
